title: RayleaBot Plugin JSONL Protocol
description: Fixture-ready schema for the RayleaBot platform-runtime JSONL protocol. This contract freezes startup, startup progress, shared outbound segment payloads, shared local actions, frozen OneBot single-action capabilities, current provider extension actions, event dispatch, result, error, ping/pong keepalive, and shutdown semantics.

sub_type.description: Event sub-type for notice events (e.g., approve, invite, kick, leave).

operator_id.description: The user who triggered the notice event, if different from the actor.

raw_payload.description: Only present when the plugin manifest declares event.raw_payload.

webhook.description: Only present for webhook.received events. Carries normalized replay-protection metadata derived from the inbound request headers and the server clock.

received_at.description: Server-side ingestion timestamp in seconds since the unix epoch.

client_timestamp.description: Client-supplied timestamp from the replay_protection.timestamp_header value. Omitted only when the route is in grace mode (enforce=false) and the header was absent.

client_event_id.description: Client-supplied event id from the replay_protection.event_id_header value. Omitted only when the route is in grace mode and the header was absent; in that case the platform falls back to a server-generated event id and exposes it in the parent event_id field.

details.description: Optional structured details shared by terminal plugin failures and platform local action failures.

replay_protection.description: Replay protection contract. The exposed webhook caller must supply a unix-epoch timestamp and a unique event id with every request. When auth_strategy is hmac_sha256, the signature input is composed as the timestamp ASCII digits, a single newline, the event id, a single newline, and the raw request body. The server rejects requests whose timestamp differs from the server clock by more than tolerance_seconds or whose (route, event_id) pair has been observed within the rolling replay window. enforce=false degrades all rejections to log-only observation and emits webhook_replay_grace_observed counts; the route still requires the timestamp and event id headers when present but falls back to a server-generated event id when missing.

template.description: Render template reference. Plugin code may pass a package-local template short ID declared in render_templates, a system template ID, or the current plugin's formal plugin.<plugin_id>.<template_id> ID. Other plugin templates are rejected by the platform.

visibility.description: Catalog returns the full plugin command and help catalog. Caller returns commands and help entries visible to the parent event actor.

x-message-catalog.description: The platform resolves a package-local template short ID against the calling plugin before falling back to a system template ID. Formal plugin template IDs use plugin.<plugin_id>.<template_id> and are only renderable by the owning plugin.

$comment: Frozen plugin action kinds include terminal message.send/message.reply, local-action RPC logger.write/storage.kv/config.read/plugin.list/secret.read/thirdparty.account.read/config.write/governance.blacklist.read/governance.blacklist.write/governance.whitelist.read/governance.whitelist.write/governance.command_policy.read/storage.file/http.request/scheduler.create/event.expose_webhook/render.image, the frozen OneBot single-action surface, and the three current provider extension actions. secret.read reads the calling plugin's secret namespace only and is separate from non-sensitive config.read. thirdparty.account.read reads enabled, non-invalid third-party accounts allowed by manifest-declared platforms and returns credentials as secret values. render.image accepts system template IDs and the calling plugin's declared package-local template short IDs; plugin template formal IDs use plugin.<plugin_id>.<template_id> and are not cross-plugin renderable. init conveys manifest-declared capabilities, sanitized command_prefixes, permissions.super_admins, and the current bot identity when available. bot.identity.changed carries the current bot identity after a protocol adapter becomes ready. management.action delivers plugin management UI action payloads to the owning plugin. message.* and meta.* use the shared frozen event model; local actions use distinct request_ids, may carry parent_request_id to identify the owning event session, and receive result/error frames from the platform before the plugin completes its event. Concurrent plugins must provide parent_request_id on local action frames. event.raw_payload remains a high-sensitivity event field controlled by the manifest declaration. Ping/pong remain the minimum keepalive semantics. Batching and complex streaming payloads remain out of scope.
